chunks:
# ==============================================================================
# Chapter 7: Project 3 — Background Job System (Infrastructure Pattern)
# ==============================================================================
# Module-level intent for the seventh chapter of the IVD book. Third hands-on
# project in Part II: build a Celery-based background job system using IVD,
# apply the infra-background-job recipe, and measure knowledge durability
# through a team member turnover simulation.
#
# Reference: book/book_system_intent.yaml (system)
# Parent:    book_structure.part_2_practice.chapters[2]
# Source:    recipes/infra-background-job.yaml, framework.md, templates/intent.yaml
# Version: 1.1
# Created: 2026-02-09
# Status: Pending
# ==============================================================================

parent_intent: "../../../book_system_intent.yaml"

scope:
  level: "module"
  type: "chapter"
  granularity: "coarse"

# ==============================================================================
# INTENT: What should this chapter accomplish?
# ==============================================================================

intent:
  summary: "Guide the reader through building a Celery-based background job system using IVD—writing the intent artifact, applying the infra-background-job recipe, implementing with retries, monitoring, and idempotency—then measuring knowledge durability by simulating team member turnover."
  
  goal: |
    By the end of this chapter, the reader:
    1. Has built a complete, working background job system (async job processing with retries, monitoring, idempotency) using IVD
    2. Can apply the infra-background-job recipe to a real infrastructure problem
    3. Has written a module-level intent artifact for the job system (scope, intent, constraints, verification)
    4. Understands how IVD captures infrastructure knowledge that traditionally lives only in senior engineers' heads
    5. Has run the "context reset" experiment: simulated a team member leaving and measured how fast a new person (or new AI session) can understand the system with vs without IVD
    6. Has measurable evidence: onboarding speed baseline (no IVD) vs IVD approach (time to first meaningful contribution)
    7. Recognizes the infrastructure pattern so they can apply it to other infra domains (queue workers, scheduled tasks, data pipelines)
    
    This is the third practice project. The reader has already built an AI agent (Ch5) and a multi-step workflow (Ch6). Now they tackle infrastructure—a domain where knowledge durability is the primary challenge. The unique measurement in this chapter is not about building speed or accuracy, but about whether understanding survives team turnover. Code and intent artifacts are the centerpiece; prose explains steps and rationale.
  
  success_metric: |
    - Reader has a working background job system at the end of the chapter
    - Reader wrote (or followed along with) a module-level intent artifact referencing the infra-background-job recipe
    - Reader can articulate what the infra-background-job recipe provides and how to customize it
    - Context reset experiment is clear: f

e—a domain where knowledge durability is the primary challenge. The unique measurement in this chapter is not about building speed or accuracy, but about whether understanding survives team turnover. Code and intent artifacts are the centerpiece; prose explains steps and rationale.
  
  success_metric: |
    - Reader has a working background job system at the end of the chapter
    - Reader wrote (or followed along with) a module-level intent artifact referencing the infra-background-job recipe
    - Reader can articulate what the infra-background-job recipe provides and how to customize it
    - Context reset experiment is clear: fresh AI session (or new team member) can understand the system faster with IVD artifacts than without
    - Knowledge durability measurement is concrete: time to first meaningful change, questions asked, misunderstandings encountered
    - Reader sees the pattern: infrastructure knowledge is the most fragile kind—IVD makes it durable
    - Chapter ends with pattern recognition bridge to Project 4 (Chapter 8, integration pattern)
  
  stakeholders:
    - "IVD book author (manuscript)"
    - "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_2 purpose: build real projects with IVD)"
    - "recipes/infra-background-job.yaml (canonical recipe)"

# ==============================================================================
# DELIVERABLES: Required sections (from book_system_intent Ch7)
# ==============================================================================

deliverables:
  sections:
    - id: "7.1"
      title: "Project Overview: Celery-Based Job Queue"
      required_content:
        - "What we're building: an async background job system using Celery with retries, monitoring, idempotency, and progress tracking"
        - "Why this project: infrastructure is where knowledge dies—the engineer who built it leaves, and the team is lost. Background jobs are a universal pattern (every production system has them) with high tribal knowledge risk"
        - "Why Celery: mature, well-documented, production-proven; uses Redis as broker; the recipe is based on real usage (Lore Fact Check Job). The principles apply to any task queue (Dramatiq, Huey, cloud-native equivalents)"
        - "What the reader will learn: the full IVD cycle on infrastructure, recipe usage, and a unique measurement—knowledge durability through turnover simulation"
        - "Prerequisites: Part I concepts; basic Python; Chapters 5–6 completed (familiar with IVD cycle); Docker for Redis (optional but recommended)"
        - "End state: working job system with intent artifact, tests, and a turnover simulation comparing onboarding speed with vs without IVD"
        - "The unique challenge of this chapter: unlike Ch5 (accuracy) and Ch6 (data integrity), the metric here is whether a NEW person can understand the system. Not 'does it work?' but 'can someone else understand WHY it works?'"
    
    - id: "7.2"
      title: "The Durability Challenge: 'How Does This Work?'"
      required_content:
        - "The infrastructure knowledge problem: most infra knowledge lives in one person's head. When they leave, the knowledge leaves with them"
        - "The 'How does th

e: working job system with intent artifact, tests, and a turnover simulation comparing onboarding speed with vs without IVD"
        - "The unique challenge of this chapter: unlike Ch5 (accuracy) and Ch6 (data integrity), the metric here is whether a NEW person can understand the system. Not 'does it work?' but 'can someone else understand WHY it works?'"
    
    - id: "7.2"
      title: "The Durability Challenge: 'How Does This Work?'"
      required_content:
        - "The infrastructure knowledge problem: most infra knowledge lives in one person's head. When they leave, the knowledge leaves with them"
        - "The 'How does this work?' moment: a new engineer joins, sees a Celery worker processing jobs, and asks: Why is max_retries 3? Why exponential backoff? Why is this job idempotent but that one isn't? What happens when the queue backs up?"
        - "Without IVD: the answers are in Slack messages, pull request comments, and the original engineer's memory. The new engineer reverse-engineers from code, makes wrong assumptions, introduces bugs"
        - "With IVD: the intent artifact answers every 'why' question. Constraints explain limits. Rationale explains decisions. The recipe provides the pattern. The new engineer reads the intent and understands the system before touching code"
        - "Frame the chapter's measurement: we will simulate this exact scenario. Build the system, then pretend the original engineer left. How fast can a 'new person' (fresh AI session with no prior context) understand and modify the system?"
        - "Connect to IVD Principle 3 (Bidirectional Synchronization): intent must reflect code AND code must reflect intent. When both are true, knowledge is durable—it doesn't depend on any single person"
    
    - id: "7.3"
      title: "Step 1: Intent Artifact with Recipe Reference"
      required_content:
        - "Start with natural language: reader describes what they want ('I need a background job system that processes items asynchronously with retries and monitoring...')"
        - "AI writes the intent artifact: scope (level=module, type=infrastructure), intent (summary, goal, success_metric), constraints (completion_rate >= 95%, processing_time p95 < target, retry_behavior, idempotency)"
        - "Reference the infra-background-job recipe: recipe: 'infra-background-job', recipe_version: '1.0' — show how the recipe provides the constraint template, implementation pattern, and verification pattern"
        - "Constraints deep dive: each constraint from the recipe template customized to this specific job system. completion_rate, processing_time, retry_behavior, idempotency — each with requirement, test path, and consequence_if_violated"
        - "Rationale section: why Celery over alternatives (synchronous processing, Lambda functions, cron jobs, raw message queues). Show how the recipe's alternatives_template guides this decision"
        - "Show the complete module-level YAML intent artifact inline (the chapter's primary teaching artifact)"
        - "Highlight: the intent captures the 'why' behind every infrastructure decision. This is what survives team turnover — not the code, but the intent"

omized to this specific job system. completion_rate, processing_time, retry_behavior, idempotency — each with requirement, test path, and consequence_if_violated"
        - "Rationale section: why Celery over alternatives (synchronous processing, Lambda functions, cron jobs, raw message queues). Show how the recipe's alternatives_template guides this decision"
        - "Show the complete module-level YAML intent artifact inline (the chapter's primary teaching artifact)"
        - "Highlight: the intent captures the 'why' behind every infrastructure decision. This is what survives team turnover — not the code, but the intent"
    
    - id: "7.4"
      title: "Step 2: Implementation Following Recipe Pattern"
      required_content:
        - "The IVD implementation flow: hand the intent artifact to the AI assistant → AI reads the intent → AI implements against constraints"
        - "Show the implementation pattern from the recipe: directory structure (job/[job_name]/, job.py, processor.py, utils.py, tests/)"
        - "Walk through key implementation decisions: Celery task definition with retry decorator, exponential backoff configuration, idempotency checks (already_processed), transient vs permanent failure handling"
        - "AI implements: show code snippets for the main job (Celery task with @celery.task decorator), processor (fetch, process, mark_processed), and monitoring (track_success, track_failure, track_retry)"
        - "Emphasize: the AI has the intent as context — it knows completion_rate >= 95%, it knows idempotency is required, it knows to distinguish transient from permanent failures"
        - "Show how the recipe's code_structure maps directly to the implementation — the reader isn't inventing patterns, they're applying a validated template"
    
    - id: "7.5"
      title: "Step 3: Simulate Team Turnover (Context Reset)"
      required_content:
        - "The experiment: simulate the original engineer leaving. Start a fresh AI session (new context, no conversation history) and ask it to understand and modify the system"
        - "Without IVD scenario: give the fresh AI session ONLY the code (no intent artifact, no recipe reference). Ask it: 'Why is max_retries set to 3?' 'What happens if this job runs twice?' 'Add a new job type following the same pattern.' Record: time to answer, accuracy of answers, questions it can't answer, mistakes it makes"
        - "With IVD scenario: give the fresh AI session the code AND the intent artifact AND the recipe reference. Ask the same questions. Record: time to answer, accuracy of answers, confidence in answers, ability to add new job type correctly"
        - "Guide the reader step-by-step through running both scenarios. This is the chapter's core measurement — the reader experiences the difference firsthand"
        - "Key insight: the code tells you WHAT the system does; the intent tells you WHY. Without the 'why', the new person can read code but can't make informed changes"
        - "Connect to the reader's real experience: have they ever inherited code where they could read it but couldn't understand the decisions? That's the problem IVD solves"
    
    - id: "7.6"
      t

time to answer, accuracy of answers, confidence in answers, ability to add new job type correctly"
        - "Guide the reader step-by-step through running both scenarios. This is the chapter's core measurement — the reader experiences the difference firsthand"
        - "Key insight: the code tells you WHAT the system does; the intent tells you WHY. Without the 'why', the new person can read code but can't make informed changes"
        - "Connect to the reader's real experience: have they ever inherited code where they could read it but couldn't understand the decisions? That's the problem IVD solves"
    
    - id: "7.6"
      title: "Measurement: Onboarding Speed (Baseline vs IVD)"
      required_content:
        - "Structured comparison of the two scenarios from 7.5"
        - "Metrics: time to first correct answer, time to first meaningful code change, number of wrong assumptions, number of 'I don't know why' answers, ability to add new job type following existing patterns"
        - "Measurement table or clear structured comparison: baseline (code only) vs IVD (code + intent + recipe)"
        - "This is knowledge durability evidence — IVD doesn't just help you build, it helps the next person understand"
        - "Tie back to Chapter 1 (alignment crisis) and Chapter 2 (IVD paradigm): the alignment problem isn't just between you and AI, it's between past-you and future-team. Intent artifacts are the bridge"
        - "Note: measurements are illustrative from author experience, not controlled experiments (constraint: no_unsourced_statistics)"
    
    - id: "7.7"
      title: "Results: Knowledge Retention After Context Loss"
      required_content:
        - "Key Points / Summary block (constraint: chapter_takeaways)"
        - "What the experiment revealed: knowledge durability is the infrastructure pattern's killer metric. Building is easy; ensuring the next person understands is hard"
        - "Pattern recognition: the IVD cycle applied to infrastructure — recipe → intent → implement → verify → measure (turnover simulation). Same cycle as Ch5 and Ch6, different measurement"
        - "The infra-background-job recipe gave you structure; the intent artifact gave you verifiable constraints AND durable knowledge; the turnover simulation gave you evidence"
        - "This pattern applies to any infrastructure domain: queue workers, scheduled tasks, data pipelines, deployment configurations, monitoring setups (recipe applies_to list)"
        - "Bridge to Chapter 8: 'In the next project, you'll tackle an external API integration — a pattern where the system boundary extends beyond your code and third-party constraints shape your intent'"
        - "Checklist: 'Before you move on' — did you write your intent? Did your constraints pass? Did you run the turnover simulation? Did the fresh context understand the system faster with IVD?"
  
  manuscript:
    file: "chapter.md"
    required_elements:
      - "Key Points or Summary block at end (constraint: chapter_takeaways)"
      - "Key Points reflect book_system_intent Ch7 key_points: async job processing with retries/monitoring/idempotency, infra-background-job recipe, knowledge durability

nal API integration — a pattern where the system boundary extends beyond your code and third-party constraints shape your intent'"
        - "Checklist: 'Before you move on' — did you write your intent? Did your constraints pass? Did you run the turnover simulation? Did the fresh context understand the system faster with IVD?"
  
  manuscript:
    file: "chapter.md"
    required_elements:
      - "Key Points or Summary block at end (constraint: chapter_takeaways)"
      - "Key Points reflect book_system_intent Ch7 key_points: async job processing with retries/monitoring/idempotency, infra-background-job recipe, knowledge durability measurement"
      - "At least one complete module-level YAML intent artifact inline (section 7.3)"
      - "Code snippets for job implementation (section 7.4) — recipe's code_structure adapted"
      - "Turnover simulation walkthrough with baseline vs IVD comparison (sections 7.5–7.6)"
      - "1–2 quotable lines where natural (constraint: quotable_principles)"
      - "Checklist in 7.7 (constraint: chapter_takeaways)"
      - "Clear bridge to Chapter 8 (integration pattern)"

# ==============================================================================
# KEY POINTS (from book_system_intent Ch7; must be reflected in chapter)
# ==============================================================================

key_points:
  - "Build async job processing with retries, monitoring, and idempotency — the third complete practice project, focused on infrastructure"
  - "Apply the infra-background-job recipe: recipe provides structure (directory, constraints_template, implementation_pattern, verification_pattern), intent customizes it to the specific job system"
  - "Full IVD cycle on infrastructure: describe → AI writes intent → human reviews → AI implements → AI verifies against constraints"
  - "Constraints are executable, not aspirational: completion_rate >= 95%, retry with exponential backoff, idempotency verified — each with a test path (Principle 2)"
  - "Infrastructure knowledge is the most fragile kind: it lives in one engineer's head and leaves when they leave. IVD makes it durable"
  - "Unique measurement — knowledge durability: simulate team member turnover (context reset). Fresh AI session with code-only vs code + intent + recipe. Measure onboarding speed"
  - "The code tells you WHAT; the intent tells you WHY. Without the 'why', knowledge doesn't survive turnover"
  - "Pattern recognition: recipe → intent → implement → verify → measure (turnover simulation) is repeatable for any infrastructure pattern"

# ==============================================================================
# CONSTRAINTS (from book intent; chapter-specific)
# ==============================================================================

constraints:
  - name: "chapter_takeaways"
    requirement: "Chapter ends with 'Key Points' or 'Summary' block and a pre-move-on checklist"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.chapter_takeaways"
    validation: "chapter.md contains a ## Key Points or ## Summary section plus checklist"
    test: "grep '## Key Points\\|## Summary' chapter.md returns at least 1 match; grep 'checklist\\|Before yo

frastructure pattern"

# ==============================================================================
# CONSTRAINTS (from book intent; chapter-specific)
# ==============================================================================

constraints:
  - name: "chapter_takeaways"
    requirement: "Chapter ends with 'Key Points' or 'Summary' block and a pre-move-on checklist"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.chapter_takeaways"
    validation: "chapter.md contains a ## Key Points or ## Summary section plus checklist"
    test: "grep '## Key Points\\|## Summary' chapter.md returns at least 1 match; grep 'checklist\\|Before you move on' chapter.md returns at least 1 match"
  
  - name: "quotable_principles"
    requirement: "1–2 quotable, memorable lines in chapter where natural"
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.quotable_lines"
    validation: "Manual: at least one line is tweetable/memorable"
    test: "At least one bold standalone sentence in chapter.md works as a tweet/pull-quote"
  
  - name: "writing_style_part_2"
    requirement: "Code and intent artifacts are centerpiece; prose explains steps and rationale. 'Build this' drives learning."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.code_and_prose.part_2"
    validation: "Manual: code/YAML leads, prose supports; reader is building, not just reading"
    test: "chapter.md contains at least 3 fenced code/YAML blocks; code blocks appear before or alongside explanatory prose, not after long prose-only passages"
  
  - name: "formatting_conventions"
    requirement: "Follow book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions: bold for key terms, italic for emphasis, code for paths/commands, paragraph structure."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions"
    validation: "Manual: formatting matches conventions"
    test: "Key terms bolded on first use; code font for file paths and commands; no paragraph exceeds 10 sentences without subheading"
  
  - name: "recipe_alignment"
    requirement: "Job system implementation must align with recipes/infra-background-job.yaml structure (directory_structure, constraints_template, implementation_pattern, verification_pattern)"
    source: "recipes/infra-background-job.yaml"
    validation: "Chapter's YAML artifact and code snippets match recipe patterns; no contradictions with recipe"
    test: "Directory structure, constraint names (completion_rate, processing_time, retry_behavior, idempotency), and verification pattern in chapter.md match recipes/infra-background-job.yaml; diff produces zero contradictions"
  
  - name: "master_intent_alignment"
    requirement: "Chapter content must not contradict ivd_system_intent.yaml or book_system_intent.yaml"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.master_intent_alignment"
    validation: "Manual review: Ch7 aligns with framework principles and recipe; no contradictions"
    test: "No principle, optional section, or framework concept in chapter.md contradicts ivd_system_intent.yaml or book_system_intent.yaml"
  
  - name: "no_timeline_references"
    requirement: "Zero timeline references in chapter content (days, weeks, months) unless in examples/code"

diff produces zero contradictions"
  
  - name: "master_intent_alignment"
    requirement: "Chapter content must not contradict ivd_system_intent.yaml or book_system_intent.yaml"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.master_intent_alignment"
    validation: "Manual review: Ch7 aligns with framework principles and recipe; no contradictions"
    test: "No principle, optional section, or framework concept in chapter.md contradicts ivd_system_intent.yaml or book_system_intent.yaml"
  
  - name: "no_timeline_references"
    requirement: "Zero timeline references in chapter content (days, weeks, months) unless in examples/code"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.no_timeline_references"
    validation: "grep -i 'days|weeks|months|timeline' chapter.md (excluding code/examples) returns no matches"
    test: "rg -i '\\b(days|weeks|months|timeline)\\b' chapter.md outside fenced code blocks returns 0 matches"
  
  - name: "no_unsourced_statistics"
    requirement: "Turnover simulation measurements must be marked as illustrative (author experience) unless backed by controlled study"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.no_unsourced_statistics"
    validation: "Measurement section includes caveat about illustrative data"
    test: "Section 7.6 contains explicit caveat text (e.g., 'illustrative', 'author experience', 'not a controlled study')"
  
  - name: "author_sourced_examples"
    requirement: "The background job scenario and code examples should reflect the author's real experience with the infra-background-job recipe (Lore Fact Check Job example in recipe)"
    source: "recipes/infra-background-job.yaml.examples"
    validation: "Manual: examples feel real, not generic; reference actual recipe examples"
    test: "Background job scenario references recipe examples (Lore Fact Check Job); no generic 'widget factory' or 'todo app' examples"

# ==============================================================================
# RATIONALE
# ==============================================================================

rationale:
  decision: "Chapter 7 is the third practice project — background job system using the infra-background-job recipe. Part II sequence: Ch5 agent (beginner), Ch6 workflow (intermediate), Ch7 infrastructure (intermediate), Ch8 integration, Ch9 multi-agent coordinator (intermediate-advanced), Ch10 complete feature full-stack (advanced)."
  
  reasoning: |
    - Part II purpose: build real projects with IVD; learn by doing. Ch7 introduces infrastructure — a fundamentally different domain from agents (Ch5) and workflows (Ch6).
    - Background job system chosen because: (1) universal infrastructure pattern (every production system has async jobs), (2) high tribal knowledge risk (the engineer who configures retries, backoff, idempotency holds critical knowledge), (3) recipe already exists and is validated (infra-background-job.yaml with Lore Fact Check Job as example), (4) natural fit for knowledge durability measurement.
    - Knowledge durability as measurement: Ch5 measured build speed (iterations), Ch6 measured data integrity (workflow correctness). Ch7 introduces a new measurement dimension — can knowledge survive

rom agents (Ch5) and workflows (Ch6).
    - Background job system chosen because: (1) universal infrastructure pattern (every production system has async jobs), (2) high tribal knowledge risk (the engineer who configures retries, backoff, idempotency holds critical knowledge), (3) recipe already exists and is validated (infra-background-job.yaml with Lore Fact Check Job as example), (4) natural fit for knowledge durability measurement.
    - Knowledge durability as measurement: Ch5 measured build speed (iterations), Ch6 measured data integrity (workflow correctness). Ch7 introduces a new measurement dimension — can knowledge survive team turnover? This is infrastructure's primary challenge and IVD's strongest value proposition for infra.
    - Turnover simulation: using a fresh AI session as proxy for a new team member is practical and reproducible. The reader can run it themselves. It makes the abstract concept of "documentation value" concrete and measurable.
    - Recipe-first approach continues: the recipe gives the reader structure; the intent artifact customizes it to the specific problem. Same pattern as Ch5–Ch6, reinforcing the recipe → intent → implement → verify cycle.
  
  evidence: "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_2, Ch7 key_points, placeholder); recipes/infra-background-job.yaml (validated recipe with Lore Fact Check Job example); framework.md (IVD flow)"
  date: "2026-02-09"
  stakeholder: "IVD book author"

# ==============================================================================
# ALTERNATIVES
# ==============================================================================

alternatives:
  - name: "Use a different infrastructure pattern (e.g., deployment pipeline, monitoring setup)"
    rejected_because: "Background jobs are more universal — every team has them. Deployment pipelines are CI/CD-tool-specific (GitHub Actions, Jenkins, etc.), which limits applicability. Background jobs are language/framework-level, making the example portable."
    when_it_works: "If the audience primarily deals with DevOps/platform engineering and CI/CD is their daily challenge."
  
  - name: "Skip the turnover simulation and use build-speed measurement (like Ch5)"
    rejected_because: "Would miss the chapter's unique contribution. Build speed was Ch5's measurement. Each chapter in Part II must demonstrate a different IVD value dimension. Ch7's value is knowledge durability — the most compelling argument for IVD in infrastructure teams."
    when_it_works: "If the chapter were targeting individual developers rather than teams, where turnover isn't a concern."

# ==============================================================================
# RISKS
# ==============================================================================

risks:
  - condition: "Reader doesn't have Celery/Redis setup and can't follow along"
    action: "Include a note in 7.1 about prerequisites (Docker for Redis, pip install celery); provide example outputs and a lightweight in-memory alternative so the chapter works even without full infrastructure setup"
    monitor: "Beta reader feedback: 'I couldn't follow along because...'"
    severity: "medium"
  
  - condi

rather than teams, where turnover isn't a concern."

# ==============================================================================
# RISKS
# ==============================================================================

risks:
  - condition: "Reader doesn't have Celery/Redis setup and can't follow along"
    action: "Include a note in 7.1 about prerequisites (Docker for Redis, pip install celery); provide example outputs and a lightweight in-memory alternative so the chapter works even without full infrastructure setup"
    monitor: "Beta reader feedback: 'I couldn't follow along because...'"
    severity: "medium"
  
  - condition: "Turnover simulation feels artificial — reader doesn't believe it maps to real team turnover"
    action: "Frame it honestly: fresh AI session is a proxy, not identical to real turnover. But it captures the core dynamic — context loss. Reference real-world onboarding pain the reader has experienced. Show that the fresh AI session's struggles mirror a new engineer's struggles"
    monitor: "Reviewer feedback on credibility of the turnover simulation"
    severity: "high"
  
  - condition: "Chapter overlaps too much with Ch5/Ch6 on the IVD cycle (recipe → intent → implement → verify)"
    action: "Minimize repetition of IVD cycle mechanics (reader knows them by Ch7). Focus prose on what's NEW: infrastructure-specific challenges (knowledge fragility, operational decisions) and the turnover simulation. Reference Ch5/Ch6 for IVD cycle details rather than re-explaining"
    monitor: "Beta reader feedback: 'This feels repetitive' or 'I already knew this from Ch5'"
    severity: "medium"
  
  - condition: "Chapter is too long — infrastructure + turnover simulation is a lot of content"
    action: "Target 25–35 pages; keep code focused on key snippets (not full listing); reference recipe and repo for complete code. The turnover simulation should be concise: setup (1 page), run both scenarios (2–3 pages), results (1–2 pages)"
    monitor: "Page count; beta reader completion rate for this chapter"
    severity: "medium"

# VERIFICATION (cross-check with book intent, recipe, and IVD framework)
# ==============================================================================

verification:
  cross_check:
    - check: "All key_points from book_system_intent Ch7 appear or are clearly implied in chapter"
      validates: "intent.success_metric"
    - check: "All deliverables.sections (7.1–7.7) are present with required_content"
      validates: "deliverables"
    - check: "Manuscript has Key Points/Summary block and pre-move-on checklist"
      validates: "constraints.chapter_takeaways"
    - check: "At least one complete module-level YAML intent artifact inline (section 7.3)"
      validates: "deliverables.sections[7.3]"
    - check: "Code snippets match recipe implementation_pattern (job.py, processor.py, Celery task decorator)"
      validates: "constraints.recipe_alignment"
    - check: "Constraints in the chapter's YAML artifact match recipe constraints_template (completion_rate, processing_time, retry_behavior, idempotency)"
      validates: "constraints.recipe_alignment"
    - check: "Turnover simulation present with both scenarios (code-only vs code + intent + recipe) and measurements"
      validates: "deliverables.sections[7.5], deliverables.sections[7.6]"
    - check: "Measurement section includes caveat about illustrative data (author experience)"
      validates: "constraints.no_unsourced_statistics"
    - check: "Writing style: code/YAML centerpiece; prose explains steps and rationale (Part II style)"
      validates: "constraints.writing_style_part_2"
    - check: "Bridge to Chapter 8 (integration pattern) present in 7.7"
      validates: "deliverables.sections[7.7]"
    - check: "No contradictions with ivd_system_intent.yaml, book_system_intent.yaml, or recipes/infra-background-job.yaml"
      validates: "constraints.master_intent_alignment, constraints.recipe_alignment"
